heading: Manuela Klenke
paragraph1: (*1984 in Cluj-Napoca) is a literary translator with a German-Romanian background. She grew up bilingual and studied linguistics at Babeș-Bolyai University and the University of Osnabrück. After working as a lecturer in German as a second language and as a technical translator (2010–2019), she has dedicated herself to literary translation since 2017.
paragraph2: She translates poetry and prose from Romanian into German and vice versa, including works by Lavinia Braniște, Florin Iaru, Livia Ștefan, Camelia Iuliana Radu, Jean Bart, Dinçer Güçyeter and Durs Grünbein. In addition to her translation work, she is active as an interpreter at literary events and advocates for the visibility of translators.
paragraph3: Her work has been recognized with several awards, including a working grant from the Deutschen Übersetzerfonds (2017), the translation grant from the state of Lower Saxony (2021), the Johann-Joachim-Christoph-Bode-Stipendium (2023), and the Radial-Stipendium (2025).
